Article Title: 'Operation Keechad': Congress targets BJP after Shinde camp triggers new Uddhav Sena crisis
Article Text:
Uddhav Thackeray and Eknath Shinde (R)

Poll Do you believe that Operation Keechad is a legitimate political strategy? Yes, it's a valid tactic. No, it's unethical.

NEW DELHI: Congress on Monday fiercely targeted the BJP over its ally Eknath Shinde-led "Operation Tiger", aimed at further weakening Uddhav Thackeray 's camp. Referring to the BJP's " Operation Lotus " and its push for a "Congress-mukt Bharat", senior Congress leader Pawan Khera slammed the saffron party and questioned the "intention behind this dacoity".Speaking to reporters after the latest setback to Shiv Sena (UBT), Khera dubbed the exercise "Operation Keechad".This breakaway from Sena UBT would be second major blow inflicted on Uddhav Thackeray's party in the last four years by Eknath Shinde , once a loyal Thackeray aide and now chief of the "real" Shiv Sena.He said, "This is Operation Keechad because the Lotus could not flower in these constituencies. Our question is: why is he so hurt that he stopped at 240 seats instead of 400? Why is he now busy stealing MPs from other parties like the TMC and now Shiv Sena? What is the intention? Is the intention to actually change the Constitution? ... What is the intention behind this dacoity?"Khera's scathing remarks came after six of Shiv Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha members decided to join Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde's camp and extend support to the ruling NDA.The development also follows another setback for an INDIA bloc party in West Bengal.Trinamool Congress , after suffering a stinging defeat at the hands of the BJP in the state, lost a major number of MPs. Twenty of its Lok Sabha members broke away from Mamata Banerjee's camp and offered support to the ruling NDA as a separate group, with Kakoli Dastidar as their leader.
Article Summary: Senior Congress leader Pawan Khera slammed the saffron party and questioned the "intention behind this dacoity" Six of Shiv Sena's nine Lok Sabha members decided to join Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde's camp and extend support to the ruling NDA.

Article Title: 'Big names involved, govt may fall': Arvind Kejriwal questions lack of CBI, ED action in Ram Temple donation 'theft' case
Article Text:
Arvind Kejriwal alleges 'big names' involved in Ram Temple donation 'theft' case

Kejriwal X post

Poll Should the AAP continue their protests over this issue until justice is served? Yes, protests are necessary to bring attention No, they should focus on governance

NEW DELHI: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) national convenor Arvind Kejriwal on Sunday claimed that "big names" were involved in the alleged Ram Temple donation theft case, adding that the government could fall if their identities were revealed.In a post on X, Kejriwal posted, "The Ram Temple holds the faith of crores of Hindus. From that very Ram Temple, donations worth crores of rupees have been stolen, yet not a single FIR has been filed. Whom is the government protecting? No matter how big the people involved in this sin may be, throw them straight into jail. Protecting the faith of crores of people is essential."In an accompanying video message, the former Delhi chief minister alleged that cash and valuables donated to the Ram Temple had been stolen and questioned the absence of action by investigative agencies."Hundreds of millions of rupees in donations have been stolen from Ayodhya's Ram Temple. It is being said that cash worth about 200 crore rupees has been stolen. And many boxes of diamonds and jewellery have been stolen. And not even one FIR has been filed. Neither UP Police filed an FIR, nor ED filed any FIR, nor CBI filed any FIR," Kejriwal wrote."They (BJP) have governments in both the Centre and UP. No raid, no arrest.They say very big names are involved. If action is taken, the government may also fall. In your opinion, who is more important to save? The government or the faith of hundreds of millions of people?" he added.Kejriwal's remarks come amid an ongoing probe into allegations related to Ram Mandir donations. On Saturday, Hanuman Garhi head priest Mahant Sanjay Das expressed confidence in the Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by Uttar Pradesh chief minister Yogi Adityanath, saying the truth would emerge through the investigation and strict action should be taken against anyone found guilty.Das also urged patience and appealed to people to wait for the findings of the SIT probe.The AAP has been protesting across Uttar Pradesh, demanding action in the case. The state is scheduled to hold Assembly elections in February-March next year.
Article Summary: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) national convenor Arvind Kejriwal claimed that "big names" were involved in the alleged Ram Temple donation theft case. He added that the government could fall if their identities were revealed. Kejriwal's remarks come amid an ongoing probe into allegations related to Ram Mandir donations.

Article Title: 'There is only one Shiv Sena, and it is led by me': Uddhav Thackeray hits back at Amit Shah
Article Text:
Uddhav Thackeray and Amit Shah (File photo)

Also Read |

Also Read |

Also Read |

Also Read |

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray on Sunday asserted that the only Shiv Sena is the one led by him, responding sharply to Union home minister and BJP leader Amit Shah 's "only one Shiv Sena" remark.Addressing a rally in Mumbai, Thackeray said, "The Shiv Sena headed by me is the only Shiv Sena."His remarks came a day after Shah, speaking at a rally in Kolhapur, declared there was no Shiv Sena faction other than the one led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde .The Shiv Sena, founded by Uddhav's father Bal Thackeray in 1966, split in June 2022 following a rebellion led by Shinde.In February 2023, the Election Commission recognised Shinde's camp as the official Shiv Sena and allotted it the party's name and symbol, while Uddhav's group was designated Shiv Sena (UBT).Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray also accused the BJP, his former ally, of attempting to "finish" his party and to poach its leaders. Drawing a contrast with his current ally, the Congress, he stated that despite being political rivals for decades, the grand old party never sought to finish the Shiv Sena."We spent 30 years fighting Congress, but it never tried to steal our leaders or finish the Shiv Sena the way the BJP did," the Sena (UBT) chief remarked.In November 2019, after a fallout with the BJP over the chief minister's post, Thackeray joined hands with the Congress and the NCP — ideological rivals of the undivided Shiv Sena — to form the Maha Vikas Aghadi (MVA) government. He subsequently became Maharashtra's chief minister.The MVA government collapsed after Thackeray resigned amid a rebellion led by Shinde, which left him without the numbers to retain power.Since then, the MVA constituents have repeatedly alleged that the BJP backed Shinde's revolt to bring down their government, an accusation the saffron party has consistently rejected.Thackeray's latest comments come at a time when the Sena (UBT) itself appears to be on the verge of a split, with the BJP again being accused: of launching "Operation Tiger." Days after reports suggested that six of Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha MPs were considering a switch to the Shinde-led Shiv Sena, two parliamentarians crossed over on Sunday.If the remaining four MPs also defect, the rebels' strength would rise to six — exactly two-thirds of Sena (UBT)'s nine-member Lok Sabha contingent — enabling them to stake claim to recognition as a separate faction, bypassing the anti-defection law.
Article Summary: Uddhav Thackeray responds sharply to BJP leader Amit Shah's "only one Shiv Sena" remark. Shah declared there was no Shiv SenA faction other than the one led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde. The ShivSena split in June 2022 following a rebellion led by Shindes.

Article Title: NEET-UG exam re-test: PM Modi waits at airport to ease aspirants’ travel
Article Text: NEW DELHI: PM Narendra Modi Sunday delayed his travel to his residence after arriving at IGI airport from Kolkata to ensure that no candidate appearing in the NEET re-examination faced any inconvenience due to traffic restrictions.People aware about this said that after arriving at IGI airport around 1. 15pm, Modi chose to wait at the airport instead of immediately leaving for his residence at Lok Kalyan Marg.PM Modi left for his residence only after the exam commenced at 2pm.BJP slams Cong for B’luru traffic jam: BJP on Sunday slammed Congress for a political event in Bengaluru’s Malleshwaram, which caused a traffic jam, resulting in some students reaching a NEET-UG exam centre late.Bangalore South MP Tejasvi Surya on X, said, “Congress could have chosen any other day for its rally at Palace Grounds. Instead, it chose the very day thousands of students are appearing for NEET.”Targetting Rahul Gandhi, the BJP MP said for weeks he has been “shedding crocodile tears over students and exams”.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi Sunday delayed his travel to his residence after arriving at IGI airport from Kolkata to ensure that no candidate appearing in the NEET re- examination faced any inconvenience due to traffic restrictions. PM Modi left for his residence only after the exam commenced at 2pm.

Article Title: 'Washed bloodstains in hospital bathroom': Parents of RG Kar rape-murder victim want CBI to quiz Mamata Banerjee’s nephew
Article Text: KOLKATA: Parents of the RG Kar rape-murder victim have now alleged that Abesh Banerjee, a doctor and nephew of former CM Mamata Banerjee , was involved in efforts to conceal evidence after the crime. They have demanded that the CBI summon and question him as part of its ongoing probe.The allegations surfaced a day after the CBI questioned three senior IPS officers — former Kolkata police commissioner Vineet Goyal and deputy commissioners of police Indira Mukherjee and Abhishek Gupta. The agency had earlier questioned former Panihati MLA Nirmal Ghosh.While a Kolkata trial court has already sentenced sole accused Sanjay Roy to life imprisonment, the Calcutta HC recently directed CBI to constitute an SIT and expand its probe into the alleged “larger conspiracy” surrounding the case.The victim’s mother, Ratna Debnath , BJP MLA from Panihati, and her husband alleged that Abesh Banerjee’s mobile phone tower location was traced to the RG Kar Hospital premises on the night of Aug 9, 2024.“According to the post-mortem report, my daughter died between midnight and 6 am. Everyone present at the spot or connected to the incident should be investigated. If others are being questioned, then Abesh Banerjee should also be called and interrogated,” she said.The victim’s father claimed “Abesh Banerjee had used a hospital bathroom to wash bloodstains from his body and that same bathroom was demolished shortly after the crime,” he said.Rejecting the allegations, a senior Trinamool neta said, “These allegations are being made solely to tarnish the image of Mamata Banerjee and are completely unfounded.” The CBI has not issued any statement on the allegations.
Article Summary: Abesh Banerjee, a doctor and nephew of former CM MamataBanerjee, was involved in efforts to conceal evidence after the crime. They have demanded that the CBI summon and question him as part of its ongoing probe. The allegations surfaced a day after the CBI questioned three senior IPS officers — former Kolkata police commissioner Vineet Goyal and deputy commissioners of police Indira Mukherjee and Abhishek Gupta.

Article Title: ​War of words between Ashtikar’s son & Andhare
Article Text:
Krushna Patil

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A public spat has broken out between Krushna Patil, the son of rebel Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar, and Sushama Andhare, the deputy leader of the Uddhav Thackeray faction of Shiv Sena, amid tensions over alleged defections under ‘Operation Tiger’.The row escalated after Krushna Patil, who is the MVA-backed candidate for the Nanded MLC poll, distanced himself from his father, who is among the MPs allegedly being targeted for poaching, while reiterating his loyalty to Uddhav Thackeray. “I am not in touch with my father,” Patil said, even as he claimed ignorance about Ashitikar’s future political move.Reacting sharply, Andhare questioned his stance and demanded that he perform the symbolic last rites of his father to prove loyalty. “Why is he trying to be smart? He is like his father only,” she said in Mumbai on Friday.Hitting back, Patil termed Andhare “mentally imbalanced”. “If I rake up Andhare’s past, she would be in big trouble. I request her not to stoop too low. Otherwise, I too can retaliate,” he told reporters in Nanded on Saturday.He also criticised Andhare for targeting him during the MLC election. “While the local Congress unit has issued a whip to support me, Andhare and others were speaking against me,” he said.Drawing parallels within Maharashtra politics, Patil cited the example of Gajanan Kirtikar and his son Amol Kirtikar, who ended up on opposite sides after the Shiv Sena split.Patil contested the Nanded local authorities’ constituency poll against Amar Rajurkar of BJP as the MVA-sponsored candidate.
Article Summary: Krushna Patil is the son of rebel Shiv Sena MP Nagesh Patil Ashitikar. His father is among the MPs allegedly being targeted for poaching. Patil has distanced himself from his father, while reiterating his loyalty to Uddhav Thackeray.

Article Title: MLA Vishawajeet Kadam, MP Vishal Patil lead protest march of farmers opposing Shaktipeeth h’way
Article Text:
Sangli MP Vishal Patil addresses the farmers at Kadegaon

Kolhapur

:





Congress MLA Vishwajeet Kadam and Sangli's independent MP Vishal Patil led the protest march in Kadegaon town of Sangli district strongly opposing the proposed Nagpur-Goa Shaktipeeth Expressway passing through the region.Veteran activist Bharat Patankar also accompanied the leaders in the protest march of the farmers. “We asked for alignment, the officials are not ready to give it. The highway is proposed only for money. We can reach the Shaktipeeths by using the existing roads. We can reach Pandharpur, Tuljapur, Mahalaxmi using the existing roads. We are going to raise the demand of the farmers to cancel the proposed highway during the state legislative assembly session starting from Monday,” Kadam, who represents the Palus-Kadegaon assembly constituency, said.MP Vishal Patil alleged that by using heavy police force, the survey of the proposed alignment is being carried out in dark. He said that the farmers have made their land fertile generations after generations and the govt wants to destroy these fertile lands.“Do not let the survey happen. If the survey is done, govt will deposit the money into the banks accounts of the farmers and will carry out the land acquisition and if required they will do it by using force,” said Patil.
Article Summary: Sangli MP Vishal Patil led the protest march in Kadegaon town of Sangli district strongly opposing the proposed Nagpur-Goa Shaktipeeth Expressway. “We asked for alignment, the officials are not ready to give it, the highway is proposed only for money,” said Patil.

Article Title: Kolhapur police tell devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10.00 am to 11.00 am to avoid likely inconvenience due to Amit Shah’s visit
Article Text:
Kolhapur





: Police have appealed to devotees not to visit Mahalaxmi temple between 10 am and 11 am on Saturday to avoid inconvenience they might face due to the visit of union home minister Amit Shah The Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. “During the visit of Shah, the temple will not shut for darshan, however to ensure safety arrangements, the devotees should visit the temple at other times,,” the statement released by the Kolhapur Police.Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis , deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt are going to attend the event of foundation laying of new Pradakshina Marg, and conservation of an old temple located in the premise of Mahalaxmi temple.Chandrakant Patil, senior BJP leader and state higher education minister said, “The Union home minister is a devotee of goddess Mahalaxmi. He has approved Rs 1500 cr from the Centre out of which Rs 900 crore will be spent on land acquisition and Rs 600 cr on beautification works. He wished to be at the temple on the first day of start of the works and accordingly he is arriving. We are also preparing for other works at the temple for which we are confident that union home minister will help get us funds. After the event, he will address rally at Kolhapur ‘Dhanyawad Amitbhai’ for sanctioning funds for Mahalaxmi temple and then at Ichalkaranji he will address rally on completion of 12 years of Prime Minister Narendra Modi .”
Article Summary: Kolhapur police issued a statement stating that the temple will not shut for the darshan. Amit Shah along with chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CM’s Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar and several other state ministers from Maharashtra govt will attend the event.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: 16-year-old girl gang-raped by two men aged over 60 near Mumbai
Article Text:
(The victim's identity has not been revealed to protect her privacy as per Supreme court directives on cases related to sexual assault)





THANE: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district, police said on Sunday.The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack, which occurred in a slum colony in Netivali village on Saturday night.According to police, the duo allegedly caught hold of the girl and raped her repeatedly.The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.The accused have been booked under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.
Article Summary: A 16-year-old girl was allegedly raped by two senior citizens in Maharashtra's Thane district. The accused, both above 60 years of age, were arrested in connection with the attack. The survivor managed to escape their clutches and lodged a complaint with the police.

Article Title: Controversy erupts after songs from Vijay movies, TVK campaigns played during convocations at Coimbatore medical colleges
Article Text:
As students approached the stage one by one to receive their degrees, TVK campaign song “Na thannanthani aalu” and TVK viral campaign BGM were played at a very high volume.





COIMBATORE: Some songs from films starring Vijay and a few others associated with the ruling TVK’s election campaigns were played during the convocation ceremonies at the ESI Medical College and the Coimbatore Medical College held on Saturday, sparking a controversy.Ministers K G Arunraj, V Sampath Kumar and K Vignesh, district collector Pavankumar G Giriyappanavar were among those who participated in the convocations.Before the proceedings began, “Raavana Mavanda” and “Oru Pere Varalaru” songs from chief minister C Joseph Vijay’s yet-to-be-released Jana Nayagan movie were played. As students approached the stage one by one to receive their degrees, TVK campaign song “Na thannanthani aalu” and TVK viral campaign BGM were played at a very high volume.As health minister Arunraj was leaving after his address, a TVK party song was played in the background. A few other Vijay movie songs and “Make way for the King” song from Allu Arjun’s Raaka movie, whose edited versions was widely used for Vijay's campaigns, were also played.This sparked a controversy with many netizens stating that there was no discipline at govt events. “This is no govt, and the administration has become a matter of joke,” a social media user remarked.
Article Summary: Some songs from films starring Vijay and a few others associated with the ruling TVK’s election campaigns were played during the convocation ceremonies. This sparked a controversy with many netizens stating that there was no discipline at govt events. “This is no govt, and the administration has become a matter of joke,” a social media user remarked.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Text:
Kochi

:





City police have submitted the chargesheet against director Ranjith in a sexual harassment case filed by a female actor.Ranjith was arrested on March 31 following a sexual harassment complaint by the actor, related to an incident allegedly took place on Jan 30. The complainant alleged that she faced inappropriate behaviour in a caravan on a movie set. Ranjith was granted bail with strict conditions by the Ernakulam magistrate court on April 10. He claimed that the accusations were false.The chargesheet against him was filed before a judicial first-class magistrate court in Ernakulam. The phone of the director was seized as part of evidence collection, and an analysis of the same has been included in the final report, sources said. The chargesheet was filed after evidence collection and collecting witness statements from others at the location. He was charged with offences, including sexual harassment, under Section 75 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.Ranjith faced similar allegations earlier as well. He resigned as chairman of the Kerala State Chalachitra Academy in Aug 2024 following allegations raised by an actor.
Article Summary: Ranjith was arrested on March 31 following a sexual harassment complaint by the actor. He was granted bail with strict conditions by the Ernakulam magistrate court on April 10. Ranjith faced similar allegations earlier as well. He resigned as chairman of the Kerala State Chalachitra Academy in Aug 2024 following allegations.
